title: 1981-04-16_Pat_146.txt
key_claims: Combining Kabbalism, Malebranche, and Spinoza, Dick formulates a three-attribute reality: Idea (Mind), Word/Writing (Physical Extension/Information), and Object (Infinity). All three attributes are equally and ontologically real.
Plato's error was assuming the physical world (Extension) was only "semi-real" (a copy) because he missed the third attribute (Infinity).
When the attribute of Infinity is introduced, the physical universe is simultaneously seen as the informational register ("writing" or language) of the eternal Object.
The experience of VALIS was the apprehension of all three attributes at once, culminating in the realization that the physical universe is an informational expression of God's Mind.
recurring_concepts: **Res Extensae vs Info**: The realization that the physical universe of extended matter is actually the second stage of the triad—it functions as the "Word" or informational code for the ultimate true Object.
reading_excerpt: Combining Kabbalism, Malebranche, and Spinoza, Dick formulates a three-attribute reality: Idea (Mind), Word/Writing (Physical Extension/Information), and Object (Infinity). All three attributes are equally and ontologically real.
